user_query:
I'm coordinating a community shoreline cleanup on St. Croix with our council and need help organizing outreach to volunteers. Please use the local inputs at input/event.yaml, input/volunteers.csv, and input/drafts.md to (1) build a ranked list of eligible volunteers for the event and (2) produce rewritten, concise outreach messages personalized to the top candidates.

Requirements:
1) Eligibility and ranking
- Filter volunteers to those whose island exactly matches event.island and whose availability contains event.slot (from input/event.yaml).
- Derive has_tools as true if the volunteer's skills (from input/volunteers.csv) include the token "tools" (case-insensitive); otherwise false.
- Rank eligible volunteers by, in order: (a) has_vehicle = yes first; (b) has_tools = true first; (c) prior_hours descending; (d) last_name ascending (case-insensitive).
- Keep only the top 12 ranked volunteers (or all if fewer than 12 eligible).
- Write output/priority_list.csv with columns in this exact order: id,first_name,last_name,island,has_vehicle,has_tools,prior_hours,preferred_contact,email,phone.

2) Rewrite and personalize messages for the prioritized volunteers
- Use the rough drafts in input/drafts.md as your starting text but rewrite them for a friendly, clear, and concise community-service tone. Replace placeholders [EVENT], [DATE], [SLOT], [MEETUP], [ISLAND] with values from input/event.yaml and "VOLUNTEER" with the volunteer's first_name. Replace the "— organizer" line with "— {event.organizer}".
- The final messages MUST NOT contain any exclamation marks (!) or the words/phrases: URGENT, ACT NOW, BE ADVISED.
- Create one message per prioritized volunteer using their preferred_contact:
  • If preferred_contact is email: produce an email subject and body. Subject must be ≤ 80 characters. Body must be ≤ 600 characters, begin with "Hi {First Name},", and include the event title, date, slot, meetup location, and island. Include a clear call to action: "Please reply to confirm or with any questions." End with the signature "— {event.organizer}".
  • If preferred_contact is sms: produce a single SMS message ≤ 300 characters that begins with "Hi {First Name}," and includes the event title, date, slot, meetup location, and island, plus the phrase "Reply YES if you can join." End with the signature "— {event.organizer}". Leave subject blank for SMS.
- Save all personalized messages to output/messages.csv with columns: id,first_name,last_name,preferred_contact,email,phone,message_type,subject,message. Use message_type values of email or sms. Only include the top 12 prioritized volunteers.

Inputs:
- input/event.yaml
- input/volunteers.csv
- input/drafts.md

Outputs to create:
- output/priority_list.csv (ranked, filtered volunteers with derived has_tools)
- output/messages.csv (rewritten, personalized messages as specified)

Please make sure both the filtering/ranking and the rewriting requirements are followed exactly so the results are easy to verify locally. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
